summary: Iteration 2 — regression + new /api/say TTS-disabled contract. All 19 backend pytest cases passed (12 regression + 7 new for /api/say). Frontend playwright flow verified: kiosk renders, emergent badge removed, SSE 'state' delivers mode transitions (idle->music->talking->music->cleaning->idle), /api/say without OPENAI_API_KEY returns 503 and does NOT flip state to talking, and the new 'say' SSE listener does not break 'state' delivery. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/say: auth → key check → pydantic validation ordering matches spec; 503 is returned BEFORE voice validation as documented — verified via test_say_invalid_voice_returns_503_first.
server.py stop_talking_if_token correctly uses _talking_token to avoid stomping on a newer state transition — good concurrency pattern.

Minor (non-blocking): playSay() calls ctx.createMediaElementSource(audio) which will throw DOMException on a second play of the same <Audio> element; currently works because playSay() constructs a fresh Audio each call — keep it that way (already does).
Minor: SAY_CACHE uses in-memory dict — acceptable for kiosk single-instance; if scaled horizontally, would need shared store. Not a bug for current scope.
seed_data_creation: None — pure API state machine; tests self-clean by resetting to idle in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All /api/say paths validated in the TTS-disabled contract (401, 422 empty, 422 >4000 chars, 503 missing key, 503 before voice-check, 404 bogus mp3 id). To test the TTS-enabled path, supply OPENAI_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env and add a test that posts short text and asserts 200 + url + durationMs>0 + state becomes 'talking' then restores. The /api/say/<id>.mp3 GET happy path requires a successful generation first, so it is only reachable in enabled-mode.
